articles.title: Sarah J Maas Expands Her Romantasy Empire with New ACOTAR Novels
articles.summary: Sarah J Maas, a renowned romantasy author with over 75 million books sold globally, has revealed plans for two new novels in her A Court of Thorns and Roses (ACOTAR) series. The announcement was made during an interview on Alex Cooper's Call Her Daddy podcast, where Maas described the upcoming story as "really, really big" and structured across four parts within three books: ACOTAR 6, ACOTAR 7, and an unannounced fourth book. She emphasized that this narrative should be viewed as a single massive tale rather than a traditional trilogy, with arcs left unwrapped to maintain continuity. The books are scheduled for release in October of this year and January 2027, marking her first publications since 2021—her longest hiatus between novels. Maas is credited with boosting the romantasy genre's popularity, largely through discussions on #BookTok, TikTok's literary community. Her influence extends to other series like Throne of Glass and Crescent City, and her work gained significant traction during the pandemic. This move to publish two books closely together is unusual for her, highlighting the scale of the new project.

articles.title: Emma Raducanu's Coaching Strategy: Balancing Independence and Guidance
articles.summary: Emma Raducanu, the British number one tennis player and 2021 US Open champion, is currently ranked 24th in the world, her highest since 2022. She is competing at Indian Wells in California and has expressed a desire to return to her natural playing instincts after feeling that multiple coaches have overly dictated her style. Raducanu's partnership with Francisco Roig ended after the Australian Open due to disagreements over her play, marking her seventh full-time coach. She is wary of hiring another full-time coach because of the intense scrutiny they would face, which might pressure her into sticking with an unsuitable choice. Instead, Raducanu plans to 'tap into a few people' for coaching advice on a flexible basis, allowing her to seek input without commitment. She is currently working with Alexis Canter and has enlisted Mark Petchey, who is at Indian Wells for his Tennis Channel job, for ad hoc assistance. This approach aims to help Raducanu rebuild her confidence and gameplay by blending external guidance with personal autonomy, as she navigates the challenges of professional tennis and public expectations.

articles.title: Lola Young's Comeback: Overcoming Personal Struggles to Return to Music
articles.summary: Singer Lola Young made a significant comeback by headlining a show at the London Palladium after a five-month break, which she took to 'work on myself' following an on-stage collapse in September. This incident highlighted her ongoing struggles, including cocaine dependency and mental health issues such as schizoaffective disorder and ADHD, as reported in profiles like the New York Times and Guardian. Despite these challenges, her career has remained strong; her song 'Messy' achieved over a billion streams globally, making it the most popular British song of 2025, and she won a Grammy for best pop solo performance and a Brit Award for breakthrough. Young's return included performances of songs like 'Bad Game (3AM)' and 'Spiders', with enthusiastic audience support. She has upcoming engagements at Sir Elton John's Oscars party and a LA concert, signaling a renewed focus on her music while leaving questions about her current sobriety unaddressed.

articles.title: Debut Novelist Claire Lynch Wins Nero Book Awards for Story on Lesbian Mothers
articles.summary: Claire Lynch, a debut novelist, has won the gold prize at the Nero Book Awards for her book A Family Matter. The novel is inspired by true stories of lesbian women who lost custody of their children in the 1980s due to societal pressures and legal biases. Judges, including Nick Hornby, praised the book for its readability, intelligence, wry humour, and deft storytelling. Lynch, an honorary professor at Brunel University of London, conducted research using court transcripts and newspaper articles to authentically portray the historical context. The book alternates between present-day and 1982 narratives to explore a mother's disappearance from her family. This marks the first time since 2013 that a debut novel has won the overall prize at these awards. The university's sponsorship did not influence the judging process, as confirmed by a spokesman. Lynch aims to shed light on past injustices and offer hope through her writing.

articles.title: Harry Styles Reflects on Life After Liam Payne's Death
articles.summary: Harry Styles, the renowned singer and former One Direction member, has opened up about the profound impact of Liam Payne's death on his life and career. Payne, his bandmate and friend, died in October 2024 at age 31 after a fall from a hotel balcony in Argentina. In an interview with DJ Zane Lowe, Styles expressed the difficulty of losing a friend who shared similar experiences, describing Payne as someone with a kind heart who aspired to greatness. This loss prompted Styles to reevaluate his own life, leading him to question his goals and how he wants to live. He believes that honoring Payne's memory involves living life to the fullest. Styles and Payne rose to fame together on The X Factor in 2010, forming One Direction with Niall Horan, Louis Tomlinson, and Zayn Malik. The band went on hiatus in 2016 but reunited at Payne's funeral. Styles admits he struggles to discuss Payne's death, noting the challenges of public grief and the pressure to express emotions authentically. After a break from touring, Styles is returning with his fourth album, starting with the single 'Aperture', marking a new chapter in his music career influenced by this personal tragedy.

articles.title: AI Detects Rare Endocrine Disease Through Hand Image Analysis
articles.summary: Researchers at Kobe University have developed an artificial intelligence system capable of identifying acromegaly, a rare endocrine disorder, by analyzing photographs of the back of the hand and a clenched fist. Acromegaly results from excessive growth hormone production, leading to enlarged hands and feet, facial changes, and organ growth over many years, often delaying diagnosis. To address privacy concerns associated with facial recognition in medical AI, the team focused on hand images, which are less identifiable but still show disease signs. They collected over 11,000 images from 725 patients across 15 Japanese institutions to train and test the AI. The model demonstrated high sensitivity and specificity, outperforming experienced endocrinologists in detecting acromegaly from the photos. This approach aims to complement clinical expertise by enabling earlier detection, reducing diagnostic delays, and improving healthcare access in underserved regions. The researchers plan to adapt the technology for other conditions with visible hand changes, such as rheumatoid arthritis, supporting broader medical applications.
